trails.name: Lake otuhie walkway
trails.description: Lake Otuhie Walkway can be accessed from Cowin Road, 30km south of Cape Farewell in the upper-South Island.

trails.name: ANATOKI BEND TO ANATOKI FORKS HUT
ANATOKI FORKS HUT TO YULETIDE PEAK
KAIHOKA LAKES WALK
trails.description: This 10 minute walk begins beside one lake and heads gently down to the other.

trails.name: RIDGE TK JUNCTION TO PARAPARA PK
MOA PARK TK - CAR PARK TO WAINUI SADDLE
INLAND TRACK - AREA BDY TO PIGEON SADDLE
INLAND TRACK - PIGEON SADDLE TO GIBBS TK
GIBBS TK-TOTARANUI TO INLAND TRACK JCT
GIBBS TK-INLAND TK JCT TO WHARI SADDLE
PUPU HYDRO WALKWAY
trails.description: Retracing an old gold-mining water race, this short walk passes through forest of young beech and rimu to mature podocarps - it's good for children but be sure to supervise them.

trails.name: BROWN HUT TO SHAKESPEARE TK JUNCTION
GIBBS TRACK JUNCTION TO MUTTON COVE CAMP
MUTTON COVE CAMP TO TOTARANUI CAMP
WAINUI CARPARK TO GIBBS TK JUNCTION
PAINES FORD TRAMLINE TRACK
trails.description: Walk to some excellent swimming holes on a track that follows an old tramline.

trails.name: Heaphy Track - Katipo to Heaphy Hut
Heaphy Tk- Heaphy Hut to Lewis SheltCLSD
WAINUI TK - BUSHEDGE TO WAINUI SADDLE
JAMES RD TO SLATE RIVER JUNCTION
SLATE RIVER JUNCTION TO BOULDER LAKE HUT
SHAKESPEARE TK JUNCTION TO GOULAND HUT
Heaphy Tk -Lewis Shelter to Mackay CLSD
Heaphy Tk-Mackay Hut to Boundary PT CLSD
MOA PARK TK - WAINUI SADDLE TO INLAND TK
Aorere Goldfields Track
trails.description: Aorere Goldfields Track is a 3-hour loop, which gives you access to explore some of the more accessible gold-mining remnants.

trails.name: Gibbs Hill Track
trails.description: Find out about walking and mountain biking on the Gibbs Hill Track in Abel Tasman National Park.

trails.name: Harwoods Hole Track
trails.description: This track leads to a view of the Harwoods Hole natural amphitheatre, the cliffs that surround the deepest vertical shaft in New Zealand. There are no views down into the shaft. Extreme care is required.

trails.name: Kaituna Track
trails.description: Beautiful native forest and gold-mining relics are the main attractions of the Kaituna Valley. To walk the whole track takes 8-9 hours, or there are shorter walks from 20 minutes to 2 hours.

trails.name: Knuckle Hill Track
trails.description: From the top of Knuckle Hill are excellent views of the top of the South Island, particularly Farewell Spit and Whanganui Inlet.

trails.name: Parapara Peak
trails.description: The track to the summit of Parapara Peak (1249 m) is steep and difficult, and requires fitness and experience.

trails.name: Rawhiti Cave Track
trails.description: Walk to a viewing structure at possibly the most diverse and extensive entrance and twilight-zone flora of any cave in New Zealand.

trails.name: Washbourn Scenic Reserve Walk
trails.description: The Washbourn Scenic Reserve is a delightful patch of forest, providing a very easy 15-minute walk.

trails.name: Te Waikoropupū Springs Walk
trails.description: View New Zealand's largest freshwater springs and the largest cold water springs in the southern hemisphere, with some of the clearest water ever measured.

trails.name: Wainui Falls Track
trails.description: A short walk through lush native bush leads to the cascading Wainui Falls, the largest and most accessible falls in Golden Bay/Mohua.
